error.signature: npm install runs arbitrary postinstall scripts from untrusted packages, potential supply chain attack

verdict.summary: npm runs lifecycle scripts (preinstall, install, postinstall) automatically on install. Malicious packages use postinstall to execute arbitrary code. This runs with the same permissions as the user running npm install.

dead_ends.action: Assume npm packages from popular authors are safe to install
dead_ends.why_fails: Supply chain attacks target popular packages via typosquatting, account takeover, or dependency confusion. Popular ≠ safe.

dead_ends.action: Review package code on npmjs.com before installing
dead_ends.why_fails: npmjs.com shows the published code, but postinstall scripts can download and execute additional code at install time. Static review misses this.

workarounds.action: Use --ignore-scripts flag to prevent lifecycle script execution
workarounds.how: npm install --ignore-scripts  # then manually run build scripts for trusted packages

workarounds.action: Use npm audit and enable npm provenance for supply chain verification
Use a lockfile (package-lock.json) and review diffs on dependency updates
workarounds.how: Always commit package-lock.json. Review lockfile diffs in PRs to catch unexpected dependency changes.
